name: UN Universal Periodic Review (UPR / UN Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review)
summary: The UN Universal Periodic Review (UPR) is a global supranational meta-framework UN Human Rights Council peer-review mechanism — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern universal multilateral peer-review-of-all-states human-rights framework (UPR established 15 March 2006 by UN General Assembly Resolution 60/251 establishing UN Human Rights Council UNHRC + first cycle commenced 7 April 2008 — formal UN peer-review-of-all-states human-rights framework, distinct from UN treaty-body frameworks which operate state-party-only frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern universal multilateral peer-review-of-all-states human-rights framework with formal universal coverage), as the central jurisdiction of the formal UN Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review Mechanism (UPR Cycle 1 2008-2011 + Cycle 2 2012-2016 + Cycle 3 2017-2021 + Cycle 4 2022-2026 + Cycle 5 2027-2031 — formal four-and-a-half-year cycle peer-review framework with all 193 UN member states reviewed in each cycle, structurally distinctive globally as the most-systematic modern human-rights peer-review framework), as the central jurisdiction of one of the most distinctive modern UN human-rights mechanism frameworks operating both formal universal-coverage framework + formal state-state peer-review framework + formal civil-society + NHRI stakeholder framework (UPR operates formal UN Member State + National Human Rights Institution NHRI + Civil Society + UN treaty-body + UN Special Procedures stakeholder framework — formal multi-stakeholder framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-UN-human-rights frameworks operating without formal multi-stakeholder framework), and as the central jurisdiction of one of the most distinctive modern UN UPR PA-relevant framework reviews globally (multiple UPR Country Reports addressing PA-relevant framework via formal recommendations on children's rights + family violence + women's rights + custody-determination + Article 13(b) Hague Convention 1980 application frameworks — formal multi-jurisdictional UPR PA-relevant framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-UN-human-rights frameworks operating without formal multi-jurisdictional PA-relevant framework). The UPR is binding upon all 193 UN member states + observer states as formal universal participation framework. The UPR is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal recommendations and concluding observations document substantial PA-relevant framework via children's rights + family violence + women's rights + custody-determination + Hague Convention 1980 Article 13(b) frameworks. The UN Human Rights Council is the formal monitoring framework body; the Office of the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights OHCHR is the formal Secretariat framework body. The UPR is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: UN General Assembly Resolution 60/251 (UN Human Rights Council Establishment)
statutory_framework.title: UN General Assembly Resolution 60/251
statutory_framework.relevance: UN General Assembly Resolution 60/251 adopted 15 March 2006 — formal establishment of UN Human Rights Council UNHRC + formal mandate to establish Universal Periodic Review mechanism.

statutory_framework.citation: UN Human Rights Council Resolution 5/1 (UPR Mechanism Framework)
statutory_framework.title: UN Human Rights Council Resolution 5/1
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Human Rights Council Resolution 5/1 adopted 18 June 2007 — formal UPR mechanism framework establishing four-and-a-half-year cycle peer-review framework.

statutory_framework.title: UPR Cycles 1-4
statutory_framework.relevance: UPR Cycle 1 2008-2011 + Cycle 2 2012-2016 + Cycle 3 2017-2021 + Cycle 4 2022-2026 — formal four-and-a-half-year cycle peer-review framework with all 193 UN member states reviewed in each cycle.

statutory_framework.citation: UPR PA-relevant Framework Recommendations Multi-Jurisdictional Database
statutory_framework.title: UPR PA-relevant Framework Recommendations
statutory_framework.relevance: UPR Info database documenting UPR recommendations on PA-relevant framework via children's rights + family violence + women's rights + custody-determination + Hague Convention 1980 Article 13(b) frameworks — formal multi-jurisdictional UPR PA-relevant framework.

apex_courts.name: UN Human Rights Council (UNHRC)
apex_courts.role: Formal monitoring framework body for UPR.

apex_courts.name: Office of the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR)
apex_courts.role: Formal Secretariat framework body for UPR.

anonymisation_convention: UPR-related individual communications operate via formal recommendations + concluding observations framework using initials where appropriate.

key_developments.title: UN Commission on Human Rights established
key_developments.description: UN Commission on Human Rights established February 1946 — predecessor framework to UN Human Rights Council + UPR.

key_developments.title: UN Human Rights Council established + UPR mandate
key_developments.description: UN General Assembly Resolution 60/251 adopted 15 March 2006 — formal establishment of UN Human Rights Council UNHRC + formal mandate to establish Universal Periodic Review mechanism.

key_developments.title: UPR Mechanism Framework established
key_developments.description: UN Human Rights Council Resolution 5/1 adopted 18 June 2007 — formal UPR mechanism framework established.

key_developments.title: UPR Cycle 1 commences
key_developments.description: UPR Cycle 1 commenced 7 April 2008 — formal four-and-a-half-year cycle peer-review framework operational.

key_developments.title: UPR Cycle 1 completes
key_developments.description: UPR Cycle 1 completed October 2011 — all 193 UN member states reviewed in first cycle.

key_developments.title: UPR Cycle 4 commences
key_developments.description: UPR Cycle 4 commenced May 2022 — current cycle 2022-2026.

structural_findings: UPR operates a supranational global UN Human Rights Council peer-review mechanism — places UPR in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern universal multilateral peer-review-of-all-states human-rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal universal coverage with all 193 UN member states reviewed in each cycle.
Central jurisdiction of formal UN Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review Mechanism is structurally distinctive globally — most-systematic modern human-rights peer-review framework.

Central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern UN human-rights mechanism frameworks operating both formal universal-coverage framework + formal state-state peer-review framework + formal civil-society + NHRI stakeholder framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal multi-stakeholder framework.
Central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern UN UPR PA-relevant framework reviews globally is structurally distinctive globally — formal multi-jurisdictional UPR PA-relevant framework.
All 193 UN member state + observer state participation framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal universal participation framework.

UPR Cycle 1 + Cycle 2 + Cycle 3 + Cycle 4 + Cycle 5 four-and-a-half-year cycle peer-review framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern universal multilateral peer-review-cycle framework.

1946 UN Commission on Human Rights + 2006 UN HRC + UPR mandate + 2007 UPR Mechanism Framework + 2008 UPR Cycle 1 + 2022 UPR Cycle 4 UN-human-rights-mechanism-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: UN UPR jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global UN Human Rights Council peer-review mechanism meta-framework (UN General Assembly Resolution 60/251 2006 + UN Human Rights Council Resolution 5/1 2007 + UPR Cycle 1 2008-2011 + Cycle 2 2012-2016 + Cycle 3 2017-2021 + Cycle 4 2022-2026 + UPR Info PA-relevant Framework Recommendations Database + OHCHR Secretariat). Only modern universal multilateral peer-review-of-all-states human-rights framework globally + central jurisdiction of formal UN Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review Mechanism + central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern UN human-rights mechanism frameworks operating both formal universal-coverage framework + formal state-state peer-review framework + formal civil-society + NHRI stakeholder framework + central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern UN UPR PA-relevant framework reviews globally + All 193 UN member state + observer state participation framework + UPR Cycle 1 + Cycle 2 + Cycle 3 + Cycle 4 + Cycle 5 four-and-a-half-year cycle peer-review framework + 1946 UN Commission on Human Rights + 2006 UN HRC + UPR mandate + 2007 UPR Mechanism Framework + 2008 UPR Cycle 1 + 2022 UPR Cycle 4 UN-human-rights-mechanism-framework-development sequence.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins global + supranational + universal-multilateral-peer-review-mechanism cluster + UPR-only-modern-universal-multilateral-peer-review-of-all-states-globally-distinctive + UPR-four-and-a-half-year-cycle-Cycle-1-2008-2011-Cycle-2-2012-2016-Cycle-3-2017-2021-Cycle-4-2022-2026 + UPR-multi-stakeholder-framework + UPR-PA-relevant-framework-recommendations + UN-Human-Rights-Council-2006 + Office-of-the-UN-High-Commissioner-for-Human-Rights-OHCHR clusters within the corpus.
